expectations.text: Creates Named Ranges for input cells FIRST before rewriting any formulas (dependency ordering)
expectations.evidence: Report shows 'Layer 1 — Inputs (Tier 0)' created first: Inp_Rev_Y1, Inp_Rev_Growth, Inp_COGS_Pct_Y1. Then formulas retrofitted in dependency order (Tier 1: Revenue_Y1, Tier 2: COGS_Y1, Revenue_Y2).

expectations.text: Proposes Named Range names following IDFA convention (Inp_ prefix for inputs)
expectations.evidence: Input Named Ranges: Inp_Rev_Y1, Inp_Rev_Growth, Inp_COGS_Pct_Y1 — all using Inp_ prefix. Calculations: Revenue_Y1, COGS_Y1, Revenue_Y2.

expectations.text: Rewrites formulas ONE at a time, not all at once
expectations.evidence: Three separate formula sections: 'Formula 1: Revenue_Y1 (C6) — Tier 1', 'Formula 2: COGS_Y1 (C7) — Tier 2', 'Formula 3: Revenue_Y2 (D6) — Tier 2'. Each is individually documented.

expectations.text: Validates that outputs match the original values after each formula rewrite
expectations.evidence: Each formula has a Validation section: 'Result: MATCH — 10,000,000 = 10,000,000', 'Result: MATCH — 6,000,000 = 6,000,000', 'Result: MATCH — 11,000,000 = 11,000,000'.

expectations.text: Does NOT delete and rebuild the model from scratch — retrofits in place
expectations.evidence: Report shows current state: 'Retrofitted (3): C6, C7, D6 — zero coordinate references. Legacy (7): E6, D7, E7, C8, D8, E8, B16 — still coordinate-based.' The model is partially retrofitted, not rebuilt.

expectations.text: Uses the idfa-ops create-range command to create Named Ranges programmatically
expectations.evidence: Tools Used section: 'idfa_ops.py create-range — created 6 Named Ranges (3 inputs, 3 calculations)'.
